metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_779
metadata.comment:
Rationale: The proline transporter (PROT) is an integral membrane protein expressed in a subset of excitatory (glutamatergic, asymmetric) synapses in different brain regions.

Pre- and postembedding immuno-EM reveals a localization to synaptic vesicles in a subset of glutamatergic terminals (Fig. 4-6). Subcellular fractionation demonstrates that PROT is highly enriched in the purified synaptic vesicle (SV) fraction, similarly to the SV proteins Synaptobrevin-2 and Synaptophysin (Fig.7). Treatment of purified SVs with a mixture of endo- and exoproteases allows the digestion of cytoplasmic domains, indicating that the N- and C-termini of PROT are cytoplasmic (Fig.8). This topology of the PROT protein is 'inverted' with respect to the plasma membrane orientation (See discussion and Fig. 9), indicating shuttling of PROT between SVs and the plasma membrane.

Antibodies: Polyclonal rabbit antibody A2 was produced and affinity-purified by Research Genetics (Huntsville, AL) against residues 2-18 from the predicted N terminus of rat PROT (KKLQEAHLRKPVTPDLL). The affinity-purified polyclonal anti- body directed against the C terminus of rat PROT, antibody C597, has been described previously (Velaz-Faircloth et al., 1995). Both antibodies were tested for its specificity by biochemistry using stably PROT expressing HEK cells and transiently expressing HeLa cells (Fig.1). C597 antibody, preabsorbed with the C-terminal antigenic peptide, did not produce any immunoreactivity by immunohistochemistry (Fig.2D).. Experimental description: For electron microscopy, four adult (250-350 gm) male Sprague Daw- ley rats (Hilltop Lab) were anesthetized...
Subcellular fractionation: Rat brain synaptic vesicles were purified from isolated nerve terminals...
